raga.name: Darbari Kanada
raga.description: A profound late-night raga known for its gravity and majestic depth. Heavy andolan on komal Ga and komal Dha is central to its character.

forbidden.name: Shuddh Gandhar
forbidden.reason: Darbari requires komal Ga with heavy andolan.

forbidden.name: Shuddh Dhaivat
forbidden.reason: Darbari uses komal Dha as a defining color.

forbidden.name: Shuddh Nishad
forbidden.reason: Komal Ni is essential for Darbari's grave mood.

movement.aroha: S R g m P d n S'
movement.avaroha: S' n d P m g R S
movement.pakad_string: R g m P, d n P, m g R S

characteristic_phrases.context: Slow ascent with andolan on komal Ga
Upper register descent with komal Dha and Ni

note_rules.Andolan: Komal Ga and komal Dha are oscillated heavily; straight intonation sounds uncharacteristic
note_rules.Ga_Ni_treatment: Komal Ga is often elongated, komal Ni is used sparingly as a color
note_rules.Pa_role: Pa anchors the upper register and stabilizes heavy oscillations

vadi.role: King note — sustains gravitas and acts as the tonal pivot.

samvadi.role: Queen note — stabilizes the raga and provides a firm resting point.

context.aesthetic: Deep, heavy, and dignified. Darbari's slow oscillations evoke a royal court atmosphere.

western_equivalent.relationship: Pitch content resembles natural minor, but Darbari's identity is driven by andolan and phrase shape.

lofi.notes: Slow, weighty phrases can add gravitas to late-night lofi.

ambient.notes: Sustained oscillations and deep mood align strongly with ambient textures.

calm.notes: More solemn than calm; works when calm is interpreted as deep stillness.

upbeat.notes: The raga's heavy gravity does not naturally support upbeat energy.

jazz_fusion.notes: Natural-minor feel with andolan suits slow blues/jazz, but gravity limits fast improv.

cinematic.notes: Majestic somber depth perfect for epic orchestral tension.

downtempo.notes: Patient exploration thrives in spacious organic mixes.

electronic.notes: Heavy andolan loses character above 100 BPM; better at slower house.

reggae_dub.notes: Low-register gravity anchors deep dub bass perfectly.

trap.notes: Grave mood clashes with aggressive hi-hats; too patient for rolls.

rock.notes: Majestic power works in slow heavy riffs, not fast punk.

fusion_notes: Heavy andolan (oscillation) on komal Ga and komal Dha does not survive tempo acceleration above ~90 BPM
substitute with slow meend or gamak in faster genres
Treat tempo as an 'illusion': even in rhythmic sections, keep melodic delivery slow and roomy (half-time or spacious syncopation).
Preserve heavy komal Ga/Dha oscillations with pitch-bend automation (or expressive sampling), not strict MIDI quantization.
Favor low/middle-register phrasing and avoid bright, fast arpeggios that make it feel like generic natural minor.
